macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Stock Index Futures Open Lower on Monday
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures fell more than 0.5% at the open, with all three major indices declining as market volatility continues amid geopolitical tensions.

items.title: S&P 500 Reaches 7-Month Low Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed at a new 7-month low last week, falling nearly 10% from its recent all-time high as the ongoing Iran conflict spooked an already nervous market and oil prices surged.

items.title: U.S. Recession Risk Approaches 50% Threshold
items.summary: Major U.S. stock indices have declined significantly in early 2026 as recession risk nears the 50% mark, according to Yahoo Finance data.

items.title: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Rates Unchanged in April
items.summary: Market data shows a 97.9% probability that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged at its April meeting.

items.title: Consumer Sentiment Hits Monthly Low as Iran War Impacts Economy
items.summary: March marked the grimmest month for consumer economic sentiment in 2026, with the University of Michigan survey showing the Iran conflict raised gasoline prices and depressed stock market confidence.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $66K Amid Recession Fears
items.summary: Bitcoin has fallen below $66,000 with recession odds at 49%, prompting a shift toward capital preservation despite $2.5 billion in spot Bitcoin ETF inflows during March. The Fear and Greed Index sits at 12, the lowest since last November.

items.title: BNP Paribas Launches Bitcoin and Ether ETNs in France
items.summary: BNP Paribas introduced six ETNs indexed on Bitcoin and Ether, accessible via regular securities accounts in France starting March 30, 2026, simplifying institutional access to crypto exposure.

items.title: SEC Classifies Solana as Digital Commodity
items.summary: The SEC designated Solana (SOL) as a digital commodity on March 22, lifting securities restrictions for custody providers and fund managers, as SOL trades around $130 with $17B stablecoin supply signaling institutional interest.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady Near $66,500 Amid Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin trades at approximately $66,528 on March 30, maintaining consolidation above $66,000 despite geopolitical tensions and market fluctuations, with steady trading volumes from institutional and retail investors.

items.title: Shibarium Hits 500M Transactions, Preps Alpha Layer Upgrade
items.summary: Shibarium reached 500 million cumulative transactions after a system update, with the upcoming Alpha Layer upgrade introducing Fully Homomorphic Encryption to the Shiba Inu ecosystem in Q2 2026; SHIB trades near $0.0000058.

items.title: Bitcoin and Altcoins Show Resilience Amid Iran Conflict
items.summary: Bitcoin and altcoins, including Ethereum, demonstrate strong resistance compared to traditional markets one month into the Iran conflict, with AI tokens performing exceptionally well and Bitcoin expected to rebound by September-October 2026.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Police Use AI Facial Recognition to Wrongly Arrest Tennessee Woman for North Dakota Crimes
items.summary: Law enforcement in Tennessee utilized AI facial recognition technology, leading to the wrongful arrest of a woman for crimes committed in North Dakota. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about the accuracy and reliability of AI in criminal investigations.

items.title: Miasma: New Tool Traps AI Web Scrapers in Endless Poison Pit
items.summary: GitHub project Miasma launches as a defensive tool designed to detect and trap AI web scrapers by feeding them endless fake data, combating unauthorized data harvesting for AI training.

items.title: Researcher Argues AI Needs Better Math, Not More RAM
items.summary: AI expert Adl Rocha suggests that breakthroughs may come from algorithmic and mathematical improvements rather than scaling hardware, potentially leading to more efficient models and democratized access.

items.title: CADSmith: New AI-Driven Multi-Agent System for CAD Generation and Validation
items.summary: CADSmith introduces a multi-agent AI framework that generates precise CAD designs with programmatic geometric validation and iterative refinement, advancing AI applications in engineering.

items.title: Nutanix Enterprise Cloud Index 2026: AI Forces Infrastructure Rethink
items.summary: The 2026 Nutanix ECI report underscores AI's massive impact on enterprises, highlighting the need to rethink infrastructure to keep pace and avoid risks of falling behind.

items.title: Dignity by Design Emerges as Key Framework for AI Risk Strategy
items.summary: New guidance promotes 'Dignity by Design' to shape AI risk strategies, alongside reports on how AI accelerates cyberattack exploit times from years to days.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ivy Road Studio Shuts Down After Wanderstop Success
items.summary: Developer Ivy Road, known for the 2025 game Wanderstop, is closing due to lack of funding for its next project Engine Angel despite seeking a publishing partner. Annapurna Interactive plans a final surprise update for Wanderstop, including a chapter select code.

items.title: Nippets Hidden Object Game Launches on Steam April 7
items.summary: Hand-drawn hidden object game Nippets, featuring cat-petting and secret-filled worlds, will fully launch on Steam on April 7 after over 100,000 itch.io downloads. Developed by Blink Industries, Frederikke Frydenlund, and Uwu Biz.

items.title: Dispatch Superhero Comedy Game Coming to Xbox
items.summary: AdHoc Studio announced during Xbox Partner Preview 2026 that its superhero workplace comedy game Dispatch is heading to Xbox this year.

items.title: Super Meat Boy 3D Release Date Set for March 31
items.summary: Super Meat Boy 3D has a confirmed release date of Tuesday, March 31, 2026, shifting from its previous 'Early 2026' window.

items.title: Star Fox Leak Suggests New Game and Movie Role
items.summary: Leaks indicate Star Fox could have a major year with Fox McCloud appearing in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie and a new game in development.

items.title: VARREL vs Nyam Gaming Overwatch Match Scheduled
items.summary: Overwatch OCS Japan Stage 1 Regular Season features a match between VARREL and Nyam Gaming on March 30 at 8:00AM ET, with odds available on Polymarket.

items.title: Tension in Australia's Gaming Industry Highlighted
items.summary: New documents underscore economic growth potential and challenges in Australia's video game industry, citing growth rates and investment returns.

items.title: Five New Steam Games Released This Weekend
items.summary: Recent Steam releases include The Wide Open Sky is Running out of Catfish, The Scourge | Tai Ương, Chained Wheels (all March 28), Dungeon Bodega Simulator (March 24), and DVD Survivors (March 26).

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Tech Mahindra and IIT Bombay Sign MoU for 3D Digital Twin Smart Campus
items.summary: Tech Mahindra signed a Memorandum of Understanding with IIT Bombay to develop a 3D Digital Twin of the Gymkhana Building in Mumbai, advancing smart campus technology. This collaboration aims to enhance infrastructure management through digital modeling.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Loses Court Case on Ad Boycott Lawsuit
items.summary: A judge dismissed X's lawsuit against an ad boycott, calling it a 'fishing expedition' and ruling the boycott perfectly legal. This marks a significant legal setback for the platform.

items.title: Federal Court Declines to Halt CPUC’s VoIP Regulatory Expansion in California
items.summary: A California federal court refused to stop the CPUC's regulatory move on VoIP providers with over 5,000 lines, imposing legacy compliance burdens. Attention now shifts to the FCC for potential intervention.

items.title: Ohio Proposal to Ban Large Data Centers Advances to November Ballot
items.summary: A measure to prohibit large data centers in Ohio has progressed toward a voter decision in November, amid growing concerns over energy and land use.

items.title: Lynas Rare Earths Short Interest Surges 36.8% in March
items.summary: Short interest in Lynas Rare Earths Limited Sponsored ADR (LYSDY) increased by 36.8% during March, signaling heightened investor skepticism in the rare earths sector critical for tech hardware.
